claim_bullets.bullet_text: Iterators are a JavaScript feature that allow us to separate the concerns of how to iterate over a collection from what we want to do with the elements of a collection. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.223-223)

Separating concerns with iterators speaks to JavaScript's fundamental nature: It's a language that wants to compose functionality out of small, single-responsibility pieces, whether those pieces are functions or objects built out of functions. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.223-223)
Out of the pieces, functions, separating concerns, and iterating over collections, we can build up more complex and powerful abstractions. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.223-223)

source_record_text: JavaScript Allongé explores fundamental and advanced JavaScript concepts, including logical operators, control flow mechanisms, and linear recursion.
